title: Product Compression Followups
goal: Implement the #341-#346 follow-up issues created from the system-intent future-work review under #230.
Make ordinary package operation cheaper without adding a new first-line workflow layer.
Commit, push, and close each bounded milestone after proof.
non_goals: Do not close #230 unless the broad product-compression lane is genuinely satisfied.
Do not introduce GitHub-specific behavior into shipped package logic.
Do not create a broad dashboard, workflow engine, or automatic alignment score.

intent.outcome: Agents can use compact package surfaces to route, review, and close work with less default reading and less durable residue.
intent.constraints: Keep the shipped package repo-, host-, tracker-, agent-, tool-, and language-agnostic. Prefer existing report/default/proof/planning/memory surfaces over new commands.
intent.latitude: Choose milestone grouping where closely related issues share the same implementation surface.
intent.escalation: Stop if the work requires deleting major public concepts or redesigning all reporting, planning, or memory at once.

execution.next_step: Implement #342 by making surface-value review inspectable through ordinary proof/review surfaces.
execution.proof: Focused workspace CLI tests, report dogfood, ruff for touched Python, contract tooling, planning checker, summary/reconcile, and git diff check.

invariants: Default report should route before dumping deep detail.
Full report detail remains reachable through an explicit profile or selector.
Surface-value and compression work must show the repeated future cost removed.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: strip away visible product residue that is no longer earning its keep

required_continuation.activation trigger: continue remaining #341-#346 child work or reroute #230 after bounded milestones land

intent_interpretation.literal request: Next ingest the new issues and implement them
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: promote the freshly created #341-#346 issues into checked-in planning and implement them end-to-end with dogfooding, commits, pushes, and closures
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: start with #341/#345 because report router and selector discipline reduce every later work loop
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject changes that merely add report fields without reducing default reading or improving selector discipline

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: stay on master; use checked-in planning; commit/push after milestones; close issues only after proof
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: milestone grouping, report profile names, exact payload shape, and validation scope
delegated_judgment.escalate when: scope expands into broad product redesign or a host-specific workflow

execution_bounds.allowed paths: workspace package source/tests/contracts, planning state/evidence/active execplan, memory package only when implementing #344
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused pytest for touched behavior; ruff for touched Python; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before changing core module ownership boundaries or removing public commands
execution_bounds.stop before touching: unrelated archived plans, unrelated memory notes, or branch state

context_budget.live working set: #230 #341 #342 #343 #344 #345 #346, report/default/proof/planning/memory routing surfaces, system-intent future-work review
context_budget.recoverable later: older closed issues referenced from the new issues
context_budget.externalize before shift: update execution_run, proof_report, intent_satisfaction, closure_check, and drift_log after each milestone
context_budget.pre-work config pull: agentic-workspace preflight, summary, reconcile, live GitHub issue list
context_budget.tiny resumability note: implement #341/#345 report router/selectors first, then #342 surface-value gate, #343 closeout distillation, #344 Memory shrinkage, #346 measures

stop_conditions.stop when: #341-#346 are implemented, validated, committed, pushed, and closed, with #230 either kept open or honestly closed
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: the slice needs a new workflow engine, dashboard, or automatic intent score
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: work turns into broad deletion of public concepts or implementation of all #230 ideas beyond the child issues
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: focused tests or package checks fail after attempted fixes

active_milestone.optional_deps: #341/#345 report router and selector support landed

immediate_next_action: Make durable surface-value review visible during ordinary proof/review.
Reuse existing report/default/proof surfaces rather than adding a new command.
invariants: Existing surfaces are preferred over new commands.
Checked-in planning state remains the active authority.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "report or defaults_command_reports_machine_readable_default_routes_as_json or contract"
uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --profile full --format json
completion_criteria: #341 makes default report output act as a router instead of requiring broad scan of deep detail.
#345 adds selector/profile discipline for deeper report detail and high-volume sections.
#342 makes durable surface-value review inspectable during ordinary proof/review.
#343 adds closeout distillation that routes learning away from archived execplans.
#344 shrinks ordinary Memory pulls through a structured durable-fact routing path.
#346 exposes compact operational-compression measures without a dashboard.
#230 is left open unless its broader product-compression acceptance is honestly satisfied.

execution_run.handoff source: preflight, summary, reconcile, live GitHub issue state, system-intent future-work review
execution_run.what happened: Implemented #341/#345 by making report default to a compact router while preserving full detail behind --profile full and top-level sections behind --section.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace CLI, report contracts, operation contracts, contract schema, tests, planning state and active execplan
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "report or contract"; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --profile full --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section effective_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_run.result for continuation: #341/#345 are ready to commit, push, and close after milestone commit.

finished_run_review.intent served: default report now routes first and selectors expose deeper data explicitly
finished_run_review.config compliance: used package preflight, summary, reconcile, report, planning checker, and contract tooling

proof_report.validation proof: Focused tests, ruff, contract tooling, package report dogfooding, planning checks, reconcile, and diff check passed for #341/#345.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Default report emitted workspace-report-router/v1; --profile full emitted workspace-report/v1; --section effective_authority emitted compact-contract-answer/v1.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Ingest and implement the new issues.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Stay quiet, repo-native, low-residue, and sharply bounded while reducing total continuation, handoff, review, and proof cost.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer compression, selectors, and better routing over new first-line surfaces.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this reduce default reading and durable residue without hiding necessary authority?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #341/#345 landed locally but #342-#346 remain open and #230 is broader than this milestone.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: active planning state and milestone validation proof
closure_check.reopen trigger: new report/default/memory/planning surfaces increase default reading or framework feel without a repeated-cost reduction

execution_summary.outcome delivered: #341/#345 report router and selector discipline implemented.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: active execplan updated with proof and continuation

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Reingested #341-#346 and promoted the product-compression follow-up lane under #230.
2026-04-26: Implemented #341/#345 by turning report into a default router with explicit full and section selectors; routed continuation to #342.
